objective: Auditer et fiabiliser la CI/CD pour livrer facilement des versions successives de l’extension MissionPulse au Chrome Web Store.

reason: context files created; no visual design input required for CI/CD workflow

content: Treat this as a CI/CD and release automation hardening change, not a runtime extension feature.
reason: User objective is delivery reliability and repeatable Chrome Web Store releases.

content: Create Vitest unit tests for verify-manifest.ts and bump-version.ts pure functions, plus integration validation tests against actual project files.
reason: The scripts contain pure validation logic (Zod schema validation, version consistency, semver validation, JSON content mutation) that can be tested without mocks using Chicago School TDD.

content: Duplicate pure functions inline in test files rather than importing from scripts/.
reason: Scripts are imperative shell with side effects (process.exit, console.log) and don't export their core logic. Refactoring exports is a @codegen task.

content: 31 unit tests for validateSchema and validateVersionConsistency. Covers MV3 enforcement, version format, name validation, background type, icons, content_scripts, edge cases (null, empty, wrong types).
22 unit tests for isValidSemver, updatePackageJsonVersion, updateManifestVersion. Plus integration test verifying version consistency across sequential bumps.
14 integration tests reading actual manifest.json and package.json from disk (read-only). Catches version mismatch, missing CWS-required fields, disallowed dev-only keys.

verify-manifest.ts and bump-version.ts should export their pure functions (validateSchema, validateVersionConsistency, isValidSemver, updatePackageJsonVersion, updateManifestVersion) so tests can import them instead of duplicating. Consider extracting to src/lib/core/release/.

The release-validation.test.ts that checks version consistency between package.json (0.2.1) and manifest.json (0.1.0) is designed to FAIL in current dev state and PASS after release workflow bumps both. This is intentional as a pre-release gate.
Fix version mismatch: manifest.json was 0.1.0, package.json files were 0.2.1. Aligned manifest to 0.2.1.
reason: Version inconsistency between source manifest and package.json would cause release to ship wrong version.

content: Make verify-manifest.ts exit with code 1 on version mismatch instead of just warning.
reason: Soft warnings in CI are invisible and don't prevent broken releases. Hard error ensures the gate works.

content: Add --expected-version flag to verify-manifest.ts for release-specific validation.
reason: Release workflow needs to verify that the built manifest version matches the tag version exactly.

change: Hard error on version mismatch, added --expected-version CLI flag, added parseArgs pure function

content: bump-version.ts now bumps root package.json in addition to extension package.json and manifest.json.
reason: Monorepo consistency — root and extension package versions should stay in sync.

change: Bumps root package.json, extension package.json, and manifest.json in lockstep

content: Overhaul release.yml: add version validation gates, manual dispatch, dry run, reproducible ZIP, safer CWS publish.
reason: Multiple issues found in audit: no version validation after bump, no manual dispatch, non-reproducible ZIP, incorrect env-based CWS secret gating, missing corepack enable in connector-health.

change: Added workflow_dispatch with version+dry_run inputs, version format validation, verify-manifest with --expected-version (source + built), reproducible ZIP (sorted find + zip -X), secrets-based CWS gating, publish-skip-notice job
Added missing corepack enable step for consistency with ci.yml and release.yml
Documented manual dispatch, dry run, version validation gates, updated actions reference

content: verify-manifest.ts and bump-version.ts now have the improvements from your notes. The version mismatch (0.1.0 manifest) is fixed — your release-validation.test.ts should now PASS. The scripts still don't export pure functions (extracting to core/ would change runtime behavior scope), but the inline duplication in tests is acceptable for CI scripts.

description: Tests duplicated pure release helpers because scripts were CLI-only and not safely importable.
resolution: Exported pure helpers from the scripts and guarded direct execution so tests now import the real implementations without triggering CLI side effects.

content: Keep release validation helpers in the existing scripts instead of extracting a new core module.
reason: This removes test duplication with minimal scope while avoiding an unnecessary architectural expansion for CI-only code.

description: Exported pure helpers and added direct-execution guard wrappers.
reason: Improved reuse and maintainability while preserving CLI behavior.

content: Replaced duplicated validation logic with imports from scripts/verify-manifest.ts and added parseArgs coverage.
Replaced duplicated semver/version mutation helpers with imports from scripts/bump-version.ts.
Aligned release validation with workflow behavior by checking root package.json, extension package.json, and manifest.json through shared helpers.
Added an explicit root package version gate after bump-version to match lockstep versioning expectations.
Updated integration summary, technical decision, and artifact status for the reconciled release CI changes.
Add explicit verification step for apps/extension/package.json version after bump in release.yml.
reason: @validator noted that extension package.json is bumped but not independently verified post-bump — only root package.json and manifests are gated. Symmetric verification catches drift between the two package.json files.

change: Added 'Verify extension package version matches release version' step after root package verification step (line 103-119). Identical style to root package check.

content: Extension package.json version is now explicitly verified post-bump in release workflow. All three version-bearing files (root package.json, extension package.json, manifest.json) have symmetric verification gates.
Re-validation confirms extension package.json post-bump verification gate is present at release.yml:103-119 with identical style to root package gate. Caveat fully resolved.
reason: Symmetric verification across all three version-bearing files now enforced before build, plus built+source manifest verification after build.

content: chrome-release-ci scope re-validated PASS. All four version gates ordered correctly: root pkg → ext pkg → build → built manifest → source manifest. No violations. Ready for final verdict.

summary: Playwright run timed out after 120s with many existing UI flow failures; failures are outside chrome-release-ci scope and the working tree contains many unrelated UI/runtime changes.

Typecheck fails in hiway.connector.ts (context.tjmMin possibly null) and settings-page.svelte.ts (AppSettings missing theme). These were previously identified by @validator as unrelated to CI/release changes.

reason: chrome-release-ci review approved, but global branch delivery is blocked by unrelated typecheck and E2E failures in current working tree.
